'''Ashley Gibson''' (born 25 September 1986) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[RFL League One|League One]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Ashley Gibson Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/g/gibson_ashley/index.php|title=Ashley Gibson Profile|publisher=rleague.com|date=2010-01-25|accessdate=2010-01-25| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100206034744/http://rleague.com/db/player/g/gibson_ashley/index.php| archivedate= 6 February 2010 | url-status= live}}</ref> He has played for both [[England national rugby league team|England]] & [[England national rugby league team#England .27A.27|England A]] at international level, with his full England debut was v [[France national rugby league team|France]] in the inaugural [[Federation Shield]] on 22 Oct 2006.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/federation-shield-2006/game-1/england-vs-france.html|title=England v France (22 Oct 2006)|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|accessdate=7 Dec 2020}}</ref>

He has previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Castleford Tigers]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]].

Following his retirement, Gibson started working as a model and an [[OnlyFans]] creator.<ref>{{cite web
 | title = Down by the Beach: Baywatch-Themed Photoshoot feat. Ashley Gibson
 | website = DA MAN
 | url = https://daman.co.id/down-by-the-beach-baywatch-themed-photoshoot-feat-ashley-gibson
 | date = 2023-04-13
 | access-date = 2025-11-21
 | language = en
}}</ref>

==Background==
Gibson was born in [[Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England.

As a youth he played for Stanningley ARLFC.

===Leeds Rhinos===
He is also known to kick goals. He played only 29 times for Leeds over four years, despite scoring a hat-trick in his début, a 74-0 victory over the [[Leigh Centurions]]. A combination of limited first team opportunity and injury halting his chances in the first team.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

===Salford City Reds===
He signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] at the end of 2009's [[Super League XIV]].

===Bradford Bulls===
[[RFL Championship]] side [[Bradford Bulls]] signed Gibson in June 2017 on a year and a half deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls/bradford-bulls-sign-wakefield-trinity-centre-1-8623496|title=Bradford Bulls sign Wakefield Trinity centre|publisher=The Yorkshire Post|date=29 June 2017|accessdate=24 January 2018}}</ref>

'''2017''' - [[2017 Bradford Bulls season|2017 Season]]

He featured in Round 20 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]) to Round 21 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]).

'''2018''' - [[2018 Bradford Bulls season|2018 Season]]

Ashley featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Sheffield Eagles]] and [[Toronto Wolfpack]].

Gibson featured in Round 1 ([[York City Knights]]) to Round 5 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.]]) then in Round 7 ([[Coventry Bears]]) to Round 17 ([[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]]). He featured in Round 23 ([[Keighley Cougars]]) then in Round 25 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.]]) to Final ([[Workington Town]]). Gibson also played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup]] in Round 3 ([[West Wales Raiders]]) to Round 5 ([[Warrington Wolves]]). He scored against [[York City Knights]] (1 try), [[West Wales Raiders]] (2 tries), [[Coventry Bears]] (1 try), [[London Skolars]] (1 try), [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]] (2 tries) and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]] (1 try).

At the end of the season Gibson signed a 1 Year extension to his contract.

'''2019''' - [[2019 Bradford Bulls season|2019 Season]]

Ashley featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[York City Knights]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Batley Bulldogs]].

Gibson featured in Round 5 ([[Toulouse Olympique]]) then in Round 8 ([[Leigh Centurions]]). Gibson also played in Round 17 ([[Leigh Centurions]]). He also played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup]] in Round 4 ([[Keighley Cougars]]). He was released at the end of the season.

Statistics do not include pre-season friendlies.

===Newcastle Thunder===
On 25 November 2019 it was reported that he had signed for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL League One]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Nov 2019|accessdate=23 Jan 2021|title=Ashley Gibson joins Newcastle|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ashley-gibson-joins-newcastle/}}</ref>

==England==
He gained national honours with the [[England Knights|England A-Team]], winning the [[Federation Shield]].<ref name="Ashley Gibson Profile"/>
